title: Active Share Lab
intro: Build a toy portfolio against a simple index. Watch active share move. Then face the judgment question: does ‘more different’ mean ‘better’?
judgmentPrompt: A study of highly active funds in popular categories finds a split: some beat their benchmark in every rolling window studied; others in none. What should you conclude?
judgmentChoices: Maximise active share — divergence guarantees alpha
Active share measures how different you are, not whether the difference pays
Low active share funds are always better because they are safer
Only funds with active share above 90% should be bought
debrief: Active share is a useful honesty check (are you paying for a closet indexer?). It is not a performance score. High active risk can land on either side of the ledger — process and outcomes still need separate judgment.
